title: lantana camara subsp. moldenkei
intro: Lantana camara subsp. moldenkei, also known as Lantana, is a flowering shrub native to tropical and subtropical regions, characterized by its clusters of small, fragrant flowers in shades of pink, yellow, and purple. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its antiseptic and antibacterial properties. Lantana camara subsp. moldenkei has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and exhibiting antioxidant properties, although more research is needed to confirm these findings. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, where it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
